notes.anchor: M. CICERO C. CVRTIO PEDVCAEANO PR. S.
notes.note: The letter-heading; a recommendation to the praetor C. Curtius Peducaeanus (PR. = praetor) in 50 BC on behalf of Cicero's old friend M. Fadius. "S." abbreviates salutem (dicit), "sends greetings."

notes.anchor: you will keep your edict and your established practice
notes.note: The praetor's edictum was the standing proclamation, issued at the start of his year of office, setting out the legal rules and remedies he would apply; his institutum was his settled procedural practice. Cicero pointedly does not ask the praetor to depart from either, requesting only fair access for his friend.

notes.anchor: most of all at your tribunal
notes.note: The tribunal was the raised platform from which a Roman magistrate dispensed justice; here a metonym for proceedings before the praetor, where M. Fadius's lawsuits would be heard.
